title: Global citizenship and sustainable development education
subtitle: An index from 0 (worst) to 1 (best) measuring the extent to which global citizenship and sustainable development education are mainstreamed into different strands of education: national education policies, student assessment, teacher education, and education curricula.
caption:
Global citizenship and sustainable development education

An index from 0 (worst) to 1 (best) measuring the extent to which global citizenship and sustainable development education are mainstreamed into different strands of education: national education policies, student assessment, teacher education, and education curricula.

The chart displays index scores for four countries—Colombia, India, Malawi, and Bulgaria—across four education strands in 2020. Colombia and India both record the maximum score of 1 for national education policies and student assessment. Colombia’s scores for education curricula and teacher education are 0.88 and 0.85, respectively. India’s corresponding values are 0.92 for education curricula and 0.95 for teacher education. Malawi also shows scores of 1 for national education policies and student assessment, with 0.91 for education curricula and 0.90 for teacher education. Bulgaria’s scores are lower across all categories: 0.73 for teacher education, 0.71 for student assessment, 0.65 for education curricula, and 0.56 for national education policies. The indicators are based on self-reporting by countries, with a requirement to provide supporting evidence for the responses. All visible bars extend horizontally from left to right, with numerical labels at their endpoints indicating the precise index value for each category within each country.
